steps.title: Identify trend direction to eliminate half the choices
steps.content: The scatterplot shows a clear positive correlation: as $x$ increases, $y$ increases. Choices C and D both have negative slopes, which would predict the opposite trend (equations $y = -0.8x + 3$ and $y = -3x + 0.8$). Both C and D are eliminated, leaving A and B.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B (y = 0.8x + 3):
steps.content:
At $x = 8$, choice B predicts:
$$y = 0.8(8) + 3 = 6.4 + 3 = 9.4$$
The actual data point near $x = 8$ shows $y \approx 26$. The predicted value of 9.4 is far too low. B fails—the slope of 0.8 is too shallow to fit the data.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice A (y = 3x + 0.8):
steps.content:
Check multiple points:
  • At $x = 1$: $y = 3(1) + 0.8 = 3.8$; actual data \approx 4 ✓
  • At $x = 4$: $y = 3(4) + 0.8 = 12.8$; actual data \approx 14 ✓
  • At $x = 8$: $y = 3(8) + 0.8 = 24.8$; actual data \approx 26 ✓
Choice A consistently fits the data across the range. That matches choice A — A is correct.
